fact: February 4 is exactly 35 days into the year, which means you've already lived through more than 9% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, groundhogs are typically still fast asleep underground right now, dreaming through their winter naps that can last up to 150 days!
February 4 lands in the middle of the coldest stretch of winter in many northern places, when the air is so cold it can freeze the moisture in your breath instantly!
Down in Antarctica, February 4 brings some of the hottest summer days of the year, with temperatures that can actually reach above freezing!
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day around this time in most northern places, adding up to 14 extra minutes of daylight just this week!
Aquarius rules the sky right now, making this the most rebellious and quirky time of the zodiac year!
Tree sap in cold places turns into natural antifreeze around February 4, preventing maple and birch trees from becoming giant popsicles!
February 4 sits exactly 46 days before the spring equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal!
Male cardinals often start singing their loudest, showiest songs right around now to impress female birds, even though it's still freezing cold in many places!
In places like Minnesota, the snow on February 4 often squeaks and crunches extra loud underfoot because it's so cold the ice crystals become super crunchy!
Ocean waves in the North Atlantic typically reach their most monstrous sizes around early February, with some waves towering over 60 feet tall!
February 4 marks the time when hibernating bears' heart rates drop to just 8 beats per minute - slower than a sleeping human baby!
